narrative: Louisiana received a DRPI score of 68.2 out of 100 (HIGH tier), based on data from FEMA, BLS, NOAA, BEA, and the U.S. Census (2010–2024). The Ridge Regression model (LOO-CV R²=0.14) estimates a labor market stabilization period of 17 months following a major disaster event (90% prediction interval: 10–23 months). The state recorded an average FEMA disaster exposure of 92.2 declarations per 12-month window and average per-capita damage of $554. Its Compound Vulnerability Index (CVI) is 0.599 and fiscal capacity stands at $9,565 per capita. The labor instability classifier assigned a LOW tier, based on an unemployment shock of +8.2 percentage points recorded during the COVID-19 period. The state's economic structure is classified as Resource-Intensive (50% service sector share of GDP).
